subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Nutrition and Recovery
description: A micro-lesson that makes Nutrition and Recovery usable.

hook.text: Sailors with scurvy watched their old wounds reopen. Vitamin C holds your healing together.

buildup.text: Wound healing requires protein for new tissue, vitamin C for collagen, and zinc for immune function.

discovery.text: Malnourished patients heal up to 3 times slower than well-nourished ones.

twist.text: Even mild protein deficiency—common in elderly patients—significantly delays wound closure.

climax.text: Hospitals now use nutrition screening to identify patients at risk of poor healing.

punchline.text: You can't build a house without materials. You can't heal without nutrients.

quiz.question: What nutrient is essential for collagen production in wound healing?
